message:
fix(distributed): quorum-aware recovery completion and replica mutation guards

Several correctness fixes to DistributedRegionRecord:

1. complete_recovery() now verifies quorum is reached before
   transitioning back to Active. Previously it would allow recovery
   completion even with insufficient healthy replicas.

2. Replica mutation operations (add, remove, update_status, mark lost)
   are now guarded by ensure_replica_mutation_allowed(), which rejects
   mutations on terminal-state regions.

3. replica_lost() uses a new QuorumLost transition reason (instead of
   ReplicaLost) and respects config.allow_degraded: when degraded mode
   is disabled, quorum loss goes directly to Closing.

4. remove_replica() now takes a `now: Time` parameter and calls
   reconcile_quorum_loss() to auto-degrade if removal drops below
   quorum. update_replica_status() does the same.

5. New helper reconcile_quorum_loss() centralizes the quorum-check-and-
   degrade logic for use after any replica state change.

Tests:
- complete_recovery_requires_quorum: new test
- quorum_loss_closes_when_degraded_mode_disabled: new test
- Existing tests updated to restore quorum before completing recovery

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
